subject: [RFC PATCH 28/45] mm: page_alloc: keep PCP refill in tainted SPBs across owned pageblocks

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch modifies the rmqueue_bulk function in the Linux kernel's mm/page_alloc.c file to improve PCP refill performance on systems with many CPUs and tainted superpageblocks. The original code abandoned candidate pageblocks if they were already owned by another CPU, but this patch takes pages from owned pageblocks without claiming ownership or marking them as PagePCPBuddy. This change allows the atomic alloc to succeed more often and reduces the number of clean SPBs that get tainted.
